name: Scissors
description: Scissors with blades shorter than 4 inches from the pivot point are allowed in carry-on baggage. Scissors of any size are permitted in checked luggage. Larger scissors must be placed in checked bags.
tsaGuideline: Scissors with blades measuring less than 4 inches from the pivot point are allowed in carry-on bags. Scissors with blades longer than 4 inches must be packed in checked baggage. All scissors should be sheathed or wrapped to prevent injury to baggage handlers.
iataGuideline: IATA guidelines defer to local security regulations. Most countries follow similar rules allowing small scissors in carry-on, though some regions may have stricter limits on blade length.
capacityLimit: Blade length must be under 4 inches (10 cm) from the pivot point for carry-on
restrictions: Blades must be under 4 inches from pivot point for carry-on
Scissors with pointed tips may receive additional scrutiny
Final decision rests with TSA officer at checkpoint
Should be sheathed or wrapped when packed in checked bags

faqs.question: How do I measure if my scissors are under 4 inches?
faqs.answer: Measure from the pivot point (the screw/bolt where the blades cross) to the tip of the blade. If this measurement is under 4 inches (approximately 10 cm), the scissors are allowed in carry-on.

faqs.question: Are nail scissors and cuticle scissors allowed in carry-on?
faqs.answer: Yes. Small nail scissors and cuticle scissors easily meet the under-4-inch blade requirement and are permitted in carry-on baggage.

faqs.question: Can TSA confiscate scissors that are under 4 inches?
faqs.answer: The final decision rests with the TSA officer. While scissors under 4 inches are generally allowed, an officer may determine that a specific pair poses a security concern. Consider packing scissors in checked luggage to avoid any issues.
